title: Hark! The Vesper Hymn Is Stealing. (Russian Air.)
content:
Hark! the vesper hymn is stealing
        O'er the waters soft and clear;
    Nearer yet and nearer pealing,
        And now bursts upon the ear:
            Jubilate, Amen.
    Farther now, now farther stealing
        Soft it fades upon the ear:
            Jubilate, Amen.

    Now, like moonlight waves retreating
        To the shore it dies along;
    Now, like angry surges meeting,
        Breaks the mingled tide of song
            Jubilate, Amen.
    Hush! again, like waves, retreating
        To the shore, it dies along:
             Jubilate, Amen.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Thomas Moore delivers a powerful performance in "Hark! The Vesper Hymn Is Stealing. (Russian Air.)"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
